name: Zipper

x.formalRole: A representation of a data structure plus a focused location, with O(1) local navigation and update — the derivative of the structure’s type.
x.compositionRule: A focus value + a context ("one-hole" path) reconstructing the whole; moves shift the focus through the context.
laws: up ∘ down = id at a valid position; rebuild from (focus, context) yields the original whole
x.encoding: Focus + path/context type. List zipper = (reversed prefix, focus, suffix); tree zippers carry parent context.
x.useCases: Editing immutable trees/lists (text editors, DOM, ASTs) with cheap local changes and undo.
misuseRisks: Using a zipper where a mutable cursor is simpler
Complex context types for deeply nested structures

def: An immutable structure paired with a focused position and its surrounding context, giving constant-time local navigation and update without mutation.
problem: Edit a position deep inside an immutable structure without copying the whole thing or losing persistence.
mechanism: Splits the structure into the focused element and a "one-hole context" recording the path back to the root. Moving the focus reshuffles context; updating rebuilds only along the path — preserving the rest via sharing.
benefits: Clean model for editors and AST traversal
limits: Context types get complex for rich trees
failures: Inconsistent context after a malformed move
myths: "Immutable trees can’t be edited efficiently" — zippers give cheap local edits
